metadata.summary: Publish Xarray Datasets via a REST API.

documentation:
# Xpublish

Publish Xarray Datasets to the web

<!-- badges-start -->

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/xpublish)](https://pypi.org/project/xpublish/)
[![Conda](https://img.shields.io/conda/v/conda-forge/xpublish)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/xpublish)
![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/xpublish)
[![Binder](https://mybinder.org/badge_logo.svg)](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/xpublish-community/xpublish/main)

[![GitHub Workflow Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/xpublish-community/xpublish/main.yaml?logo=github)](https://github.com/xpublish-community/xpublish/actions/workflows/main.yaml)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/xpublish/badge/?version=latest)](https://xpublish.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)
[![](https://codecov.io/gh/xpublish-community/xpublish/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/xpublish-community/xpublish)
[![pre-commit.ci status](https://results.pre-commit.ci/badge/github/xpublish-community/xpublish/main.svg)](https://results.pre-commit.ci/latest/github/xpublish-community/xpublish/main)

<!-- badges-end -->

## A quick example

**Serverside: Publish a Xarray Dataset through a rest API**

<!-- server-example-start -->

```python
ds.rest.serve(host="0.0.0.0", port=9000)
```

<!-- server-example-end -->

**Client-side: Connect to a published dataset**

<!-- client-example-start -->

The published datasets can be accessed from various kinds of client applications, e.g., from within Python using Zarr and fsspec.

```python
import xarray as xr
import zarr
from fsspec.implementations.http import HTTPFileSystem

fs = HTTPFileSystem()
http_map = fs.get_mapper("http://0.0.0.0:9000/zarr/")

# open as a zarr group
zg = zarr.open_consolidated(http_map, mode="r")

# or open as another Xarray Dataset
ds = xr.open_zarr(http_map, consolidated=True)
```

Or to explore other access methods, open [http://0.0.0.0:9000/docs](http://0.0.0.0:9000/docs) in a browser.

<!-- client-example-end -->

## Why?

Xpublish lets you serve/share/publish Xarray Datasets via a web application.

The data and/or metadata in the Xarray Datasets can be exposed in various forms through [pluggable REST API endpoints](https://xpublish.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user-guide/plugins.html).
Efficient, on-demand delivery of large datasets may be enabled with Dask on the server-side.

Xpublish's [plugin ecosystem](https://xpublish.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ecosystem/index.html#plugins) has capabilities including:

- publish on-demand or derived data products
- turning xarray objects into streaming services (e.g. OPeNDAP)

## How?

Under the hood, Xpublish is using a web app (FastAPI) that is exposing a
REST-like API with builtin and/or user-defined endpoints.

For example, Xpublish provides by default a minimal Zarr compatible REST-like API with the following endpoints:

- `zarr/.zmetadata`: returns Zarr-formatted metadata keys as json strings.
- `zarr/var/0.0.0`: returns a variable data chunk as a binary string.

Futher endpoints can be added by installing or writing plugins.

modules.name: xpublish.dependencies
documentation: Helper functions to use a FastAPI dependencies.

functions.name: xpublish.dependencies.get_dataset_ids
documentation:
FastAPI dependency for getting the list of ids (string keys)
of the collection of datasets being served.

Use this callable as dependency in any FastAPI path operation
function where you need access to those ids.

This dummy dependency will be overridden when creating the FastAPI
application.

Returns:
    A list of unique keys for datasets

functions.name: xpublish.dependencies.get_dataset
documentation:
FastAPI dependency for accessing the published xarray dataset object.

Use this callable as dependency in any FastAPI path operation
function where you need access to the xarray Dataset being served.

This dummy dependency will be overridden when creating the FastAPI
application.

Parameters:
    dataset_id:
        Unique path-safe key identifying dataset

Returns:
    Requested Xarray dataset

functions.name: xpublish.dependencies.get_cache
documentation:
FastAPI dependency for accessing the application's cache.

Use this callable as dependency in any FastAPI path operation
function where you need access to the cache provided with the
application.

This dummy dependency will be overridden when creating the FastAPI
application.

functions.name: xpublish.dependencies.get_zvariables
documentation: FastAPI dependency that returns a dictionary of zarr encoded variables.

functions.name: xpublish.dependencies.get_zmetadata
documentation: FastAPI dependency that returns a consolidated zmetadata dictionary.

functions.name: xpublish.dependencies.get_plugins
documentation:
FastAPI dependency that returns the a dictionary of loaded plugins

Returns:
    Dictionary of names to initialized plugins.

functions.name: xpublish.dependencies.get_plugin_manager
documentation: Return the active plugin manager

classes.name: xpublish.plugins.included.dataset_info.DatasetInfoPlugin
documentation: Dataset metadata

modules.name: xpublish.plugins.included.module_version
documentation: Version information router

classes.name: xpublish.plugins.included.module_version.ModuleVersionPlugin
documentation: Share the currently loaded versions of key libraries

classes.name: xpublish.accessor.RestAccessor

methods.name: xpublish.accessor.RestAccessor.__call__
documentation:
Initialize this accessor by setting optional configuration values.

# Parameters

kwargs

: Arguments passed to {func}`xpublish.SingleDatasetRest.__init__`.

# Notes

This method can only be invoked once.

methods.name: xpublish.accessor.RestAccessor.cache
documentation: Returns the {class}`cachey.Cache` instance used by the FastAPI application.

methods.name: xpublish.accessor.RestAccessor.app
documentation: Returns the {class}`fastapi.FastAPI` application instance.

methods.name: xpublish.accessor.RestAccessor.serve
documentation:
Serve this FastAPI application via {func}`uvicorn.run`.

# Parameters

kwargs :

: Arguments passed to {func}`xpublish.SingleDatasetRest.serve`.

# Notes

This method is blocking and does not return.

REST API Accessor for serving one dataset in its
dedicated FastAPI application.

modules.name: xpublish.plugins.included.plugin_info
documentation: Plugin information router

classes.name: xpublish.plugins.included.plugin_info.PluginInfoPlugin
documentation: Expose plugin source and version

classes.name: xpublish.plugins.included.zarr.ZarrPlugin
documentation: Adds Zarr-like accessing endpoints for datasets

modules.name: xpublish.plugins.manage
documentation: Load and configure Xpublish plugins from entry point group `xpublish.plugin`

functions.name: xpublish.plugins.manage.find_default_plugins
documentation:
Find Xpublish plugins from entry point group `xpublish.plugin`

Individual plugins may be ignored by adding them to `exclude_plugins`.

functions.name: xpublish.plugins.manage.load_default_plugins
documentation: Find and initialize plugins from entry point group `xpublish.plugin`

functions.name: xpublish.plugins.manage.configure_plugins
documentation: Initialize and configure plugins with given dictionary of configurations

classes.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.Dependencies

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.Dependencies.__hash__
documentation: Dependency functions aren't easy to hash

A set of dependencies that are passed into plugin routers.

Some routers may be 'borrowed' by other routers to expose different
geometries of data, thus the default dependencies may need to be overridden.
By depending on the passed in version of this class, the dependencies
can be overridden predictably.

classes.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.Plugin

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.Plugin.__hash__
documentation: Make sure that the plugin is hashable to load with pluggy

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.Plugin.__dir__
documentation:
We need to override the dir as pluggy will otherwise try to inspect it,
and Pydantic has marked it class only

https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/pull/1466

Xpublish plugins provide ways to extend the core of xpublish with
new routers and other functionality.

To create a plugin, subclass `Plugin` and add attributes that are
subclasses of `PluginType` (`Router` for instance).

The specific attributes correspond to how Xpublish should use
the plugin.

classes.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.PluginSpec

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.PluginSpec.app_router
documentation:
Create an app (top-level) router for the plugin

Implementations should return an APIRouter, and define
app_router_prefix, and app_router_tags on the class,
and use those to initialize the router.

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.PluginSpec.dataset_router
documentation:
Create a dataset router for the plugin

Implementations should return an APIRouter, and define
dataset_router_prefix, and dataset_router_tags on the class,
and use those to initialize the router.

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.PluginSpec.get_datasets
documentation: Return an iterable of dataset ids that the plugin can provide

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.PluginSpec.get_dataset
documentation:
Return a dataset by requested dataset_id.

If the plugin does not have the dataset, return None

methods.name: xpublish.plugins.hooks.PluginSpec.register_hookspec
documentation: Return additional hookspec class to register with the plugin manager

Plugin extension points

Plugins do not need to implement all of the methods defined here,
instead they implement

modules.name: xpublish.routers.common
documentation: Dataset-independent API routes.

functions.name: xpublish.routers.common.get_dataset_collection_keys
documentation: Return all the currently known Dataset IDs

classes.name: xpublish.utils.cache.CostTimer
documentation: Context manager to measure wall time

functions.name: xpublish.utils.api.normalize_datasets
documentation:
Normalize the given collection of datasets.

- raise TypeError if objects other than xarray.Dataset are found
- return an empty dictionary in the special case where a single dataset is given
- convert all keys (dataset ids) to strings
- add dataset ids to their corresponding dataset object as global attribute
  (so that it can be easily retrieved within path operation functions).

functions.name: xpublish.utils.api.normalize_app_routers
documentation:
Normalise the given list of (dataset-specific) API routers.

Add or prepend `prefix` to all routers.

classes.name: xpublish.utils.api.SingleDatasetOpenAPIOverrider
documentation:
Used to override the FastAPI application openapi specs when a single
dataset is published.

In this case, the "dataset_id" path parameter is not present in API
endpoints and has to be removed manually.

See:

- https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/extending-openapi/
- https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1594

modules.name: xpublish.utils.info
documentation:
Utility functions for printing version information, adapted from
xarray/util/print_versions.py

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr._extract_dataset_zattrs
documentation: helper function to create zattrs dictionary from Dataset global attrs

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr._extract_dataarray_zattrs
documentation: helper function to extract zattrs dictionary from DataArray

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr._extract_dataarray_coords
documentation: helper function to extract coords from DataArray into a directionary

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr._extract_fill_value
documentation: helper function to extract fill value from DataArray.

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr._extract_zarray
documentation: helper function to extract zarr array metadata.

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr.create_zvariables
documentation: Helper function to create a dictionary of zarr encoded variables.

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr.create_zmetadata
documentation: Helper function to create a consolidated zmetadata dictionary.

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr.jsonify_zmetadata
documentation:
Helper function to convert zmetadata dictionary to a json
compatible dictionary.

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr.encode_chunk
documentation: helper function largely copied from zarr.Array

functions.name: xpublish.utils.zarr.get_data_chunk
documentation:
Get one chunk of data from this DataArray (da).

If this is an incomplete edge chunk, pad the returned array to match out_shape.

classes.name: xpublish.rest.Rest

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.setup_datasets
documentation:
Initialize datasets and dataset accessor function

Returns:
    Prefix for dataset routers

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.get_datasets_from_plugins
documentation:
Return dataset ids from directly loaded datasets and plugins

Used as a FastAPI dependency in dataset router plugins
via {meth}`Rest.dependencies`.

Returns:

: Dataset IDs from plugins and datasets loaded into
  {class}`xpublish.Rest` at initialization.

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.get_dataset_from_plugins
documentation:
Attempt to load dataset from plugins, otherwise return dataset from passed in dictionary of datasets

Parameters:
: dataset_id:

  : Unique key of dataset to attempt to load from plugins or
    those provided to {class}`xpublish.Rest` at initialization.

Returns:

: Dataset for selected `dataset_id`

Raises:

: FastAPI.HTTPException: When a dataset is not found a 404 error is returned.

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.setup_plugins
documentation:
Initialize and load plugins from entry_points unless explicitly provided

Parameters:
    plugins:
        If a dictionary of initialized plugins is provided,
        then the automatic loading of plugins is disabled.

        Providing an empty dictionary will also disable
        automatic loading of plugins.

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.register_plugin
documentation:
Register a plugin with the xpublish system

Args:
    plugin: Instantiated Plugin object
    plugin_name: Plugin name
    overwrite: If a plugin of the same name exist,
        setting this to True will remove the existing plugin before
        registering the new plugin. Defaults to False.

Raises:
    AttributeError: Plugin can not be registered
    ValueError: Plugin already registered, try setting overwrite to True

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.init_cache_kwargs
documentation: Set up cache kwargs

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.init_app_kwargs
documentation: Set up FastAPI application kwargs

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.cache
documentation: Returns the {class}`cachey.Cache` instance used by the FastAPI application.

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.plugins
documentation: Returns the loaded plugins

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest._init_routers
documentation: Setup plugin and dataset routers. Needs to run after dataset and plugin setup

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.plugin_routers
documentation:
Load the app and dataset routers for plugins

Returns:
    A tuple containing a list of top-level routers from plugins
    and a list of per-dataset routers from plugins

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.dependencies
documentation: FastAPI dependencies to pass to plugin router methods

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest._init_dependencies
documentation: Initialize dependencies

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest._init_app
documentation: Initiate the FastAPI application.

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.app
documentation:
Returns the {class}`fastapi.FastAPI` application instance.

# Notes

Plugins registered with {meth}`xpublish.Rest.register_plugin` after {meth}`xpublish.Rest.app`
is accessed or {meth}`xpublish.Rest.serve` is called once may not take effect.

methods.name: xpublish.rest.Rest.serve
documentation:
Serve this FastAPI application via {func}`uvicorn.run`.

# Parameters

host :

: Bind socket to this host.

port :

: Bind socket to this port.

log_level :

: App logging level, valid options are
  {'critical', 'error', 'warning', 'info', 'debug', 'trace'}.

kwargs :

: Additional arguments to be passed to {func}`uvicorn.run`.

# Notes

This method is blocking and does not return.

Used to publish multiple Xarray Datasets via a REST API (FastAPI application).

To publish a single dataset via its own FastAPI application, you might
want to use the {attr}`xarray.Dataset.rest` accessor for more convenience.
Additionally the {class}`xpublish.SingleDatasetRest` class allows has
a simplified interface for single dataset access.

# Parameters

datasets :

: A mapping of datasets objects to be served. If a mapping is given, keys
  are used as dataset ids and are converted to strings. See also the notes below.

routers :

: A list of dataset-specific {class}`fastapi.APIRouter` instances to
  include in the fastAPI application. These routers are in addition
  to any loaded via plugins.
  The items of the list may also be tuples with the following format:
  `[(router1, {'prefix': '/foo', 'tags': ['foo', 'bar']})]`, where
  the 1st tuple element is a {class}`fastapi.APIRouter` instance and the
  2nd element is a dictionary that is used to pass keyword arguments to
  {meth}`fastapi.FastAPI.include_router`.

cache_kws :

: Dictionary of keyword arguments to be passed to
  {meth}`cachey.Cache.__init__()`.
  By default, the cache size is set to 1MB, but this can be changed with
  `available_bytes`.

app_kws :

: Dictionary of keyword arguments to be passed to
  {meth}`fastapi.FastAPI.__init__()`.

plugins :

: Optional dictionary of loaded, configured plugins.
  Overrides automatic loading of plugins.
  If no plugins are desired, set to an empty dict.

# Notes

The urls of the application's API endpoints differ whether a single dataset
or a mapping (collection) of datasets is given. In the latter case, all
dataset-specific endpoint urls have the prefix `/datasets/{dataset_id}`,
where `{dataset_id}` corresponds to the keys of the mapping (converted to
strings). Still in the latter case, the endpoint `/datasets` is added and returns
the list of all dataset ids.

classes.name: xpublish.rest.SingleDatasetRest

methods.name: xpublish.rest.SingleDatasetRest.setup_datasets
documentation:
Modifies the dataset loading to instead connect to the
single dataset

Used to publish a single Xarray dataset via a REST API (FastAPI application).
Use {class}`xpublish.Rest` to publish multiple datasets.

# Parameters:

dataset :

: A single {class}`xarray.Dataset` object to be served.
